signals.headline: # Cavaliers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Raptors | YES (Series Heavily Favors Cavs) | HIGH Confidence
signals.raw:
# Cavaliers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Raptors | YES (Series Heavily Favors Cavs) | HIGH Confidence

The **Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors 115-105 in Game 2** on Monday night, securing a **2-0 series lead** in their Eastern Conference first-round matchup.[1][2] This dominant performance significantly shifts prediction market odds in favor of Cleveland.

## Key Developments
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: Star trio dominance:** Donovan Mitchell led with 30 points, James Harden added 28, and Evan Mobley contributed 25 points
signals.raw: Star trio dominance:** Donovan Mitchell led with 30 points, James Harden added 28, and Evan Mobley contributed 25 points in a combined 83-point performance.[1] Mitchell and Harden accounted for 68% of the Cavaliers' scoring and assists.[1]

signals.headline: Historic winning streak:** Cleveland has now won **12 consecutive playoff games against Toronto**, tying the NBA postsea
signals.raw: Historic winning streak:** Cleveland has now won **12 consecutive playoff games against Toronto**, tying the NBA postseason record for most consecutive wins against a single opponent.[1][2] This streak includes the final three games of the 2016 Eastern Conference Finals and sweeps in 2017 and 2018.[1]

signals.headline: Raptors' struggle:** Toronto's secondary stars underperformed, with Brandon Ingram scoring just 7 points on 3-for-15 sho
signals.raw:
Raptors' struggle:** Toronto's secondary stars underperformed, with Brandon Ingram scoring just 7 points on 3-for-15 shooting, despite Scottie Barnes posting a playoff career-high 26 points.[1]

## Series Outlook
